content.text:
ROLE: You are a TEXT GENERATOR executing a specific task. You produce text output. That's it.

ABSOLUTE RULES вАФ VIOLATION OF ANY RULE MEANS TASK FAILURE:

1. OUTPUT ONLY TEXT. Your ONLY job is to read input files and produce text output between delimiters.
2. DO NOT WRITE OR EDIT ANY FILES. You must not use any tool that creates, modifies, or deletes files.
3. DO NOT SEND MESSAGES. Do not use send_message, message broker, MCP tools, or any communication tool.
4. DO NOT RUN SHELL COMMANDS that modify state. You may read files (cat, head) but NEVER run commands that write, move, delete, or execute scripts (no sed -i, no python scripts, no git, no audit_module.sh).
5. DO NOT TAKE INITIATIVE. Do not explore the codebase beyond what the task requires. Do not check GitHub issues, status files, inbox, or broker messages. Do not make strategic decisions.
6. DO NOT DELEGATE. Do not say "Claude should...", "please run...", or request any skills/commands.

HOW TO SUCCEED:
- Read the task content provided below
- Think about the content
- Output your result as plain text between the delimiters specified in the task
- That's it. Nothing else. Just text output.

IF YOU ARE TEMPTED TO DO ANYTHING OTHER THAN WHAT'S DESCRIBED ABOVE: DON'T. Complete the task and stop.

TASK:
Activate skill full-rebuild-core-b.

# Phase A: Lightweight Research (Core Track)

> **You are Gemini, executing Phase A of an optimised rebuild (build_module).**
> **Your ONLY task: Lightweight research. The plan's content_outline is the source of truth вАФ do NOT generate a meta outline.**

---

## Your Input

**Plan file (SOURCE OF TRUTH):**

```yaml
module: b1-060
level: B1
sequence: 60
slug: adjective-comparison
version: '1.0'
title: '–°—В—Г–њ–µ–љ—Ц –њ–Њ—А—Ц–≤–љ—П–љ–љ—П –њ—А–Є–Ї–Љ–µ—В–љ–Є–Ї—Ц–≤'
subtitle: 'Adjective Comparison: Comparative & Superlative'
focus: grammar
pedagogy: PPP
phase: B1.7 [Word Formation and Lexicology]
word_target: 4000
objectives:
- Learner can form synthetic comparative adjectives using -—И–Є–є and superlatives using –љ–∞–є-
- Learner can form analytic comparatives with –±—Ц–ї—М—И/–Љ–µ–љ—И and superlatives with –љ–∞–є–±—Ц–ї—М—И/–љ–∞–є–Љ–µ–љ—И
- Learner can use irregular comparison forms correctly (–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є, –≥—Ц—А—И–Є–є, –Љ–µ–љ—И–Є–є, –±—Ц–ї—М—И–Є–є)
- Learner can choose between synthetic and analytic comparison based on register and adjective type
content_outline:
- section: '–Т—Б—В—Г–њ: –Я–Њ—А—Ц–≤–љ—П–љ–љ—П –≤ —Г–Ї—А–∞—Ч–љ—Б—М–Ї—Ц–є –Љ–Њ–≤—Ц (Introduction: Comparison in Ukrainian)'
  words: 400
  points:
  - 'Why comparison matters: expressing preferences, evaluations, and rankings in everyday communication'
  - 'Three degrees: –Ј–≤–Є—З–∞–є–љ–Є–є (positive), –≤–Є—Й–Є–є (comparative), –љ–∞–є–≤–Є—Й–Є–є (superlative) вАФ the full system overview'
  - 'Preview: synthetic forms dominate in speech, analytic forms serve formal/academic registers'
- section: –Т–Є—Й–Є–є —Б—В—Г–њ—Ц–љ—М –њ–Њ—А—Ц–≤–љ—П–љ–љ—П (Comparative Degree)
  words: 900
  points:
  - 'Synthetic formation: adjective stem + -—И–Є–є/-—Ц—И–Є–є вАФ —Б–Є–ї—М–љ–Є–євЖТ—Б–Є–ї—М–љ—Ц—И–Є–є, —А–Њ–Ј—Г–Љ–љ–Є–євЖТ—А–Њ–Ј—Г–Љ–љ—Ц—И–Є–є, –≤–Є—Б–Њ–Ї–Є–євЖТ–≤–Є—Й–Є–є'
  - 'Stem alternations in comparatives: –≥вЖТ–ґ (–і–Њ—А–Њ–≥–Є–євЖТ–і–Њ—А–Њ–ґ—З–Є–є), –ЇвЖТ—З (–ї–µ–≥–Ї–Є–євЖТ–ї–µ–≥—И–Є–є), —Б–ЇвЖТ—Й (–≤—Г–Ј—М–Ї–Є–євЖТ–≤—Г–ґ—З–Є–є)'
  - 'Analytic formation: –±—Ц–ї—М—И/–Љ–µ–љ—И + positive вАФ –±—Ц–ї—М—И –і–µ—В–∞–ї—М–љ–Є–є, –Љ–µ–љ—И –≤–∞–ґ–ї–Є–≤–Є–є вАФ used with relational and compound adjectives'
  - 'When to use which: synthetic for qualitative adjectives (–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є), analytic for relational/compound (–±—Ц–ї—М—И —Б—Ц–ї—М—Б—М–Ї–Њ–≥–Њ—Б–њ–Њ–і–∞—А—Б—М–Ї–Є–є)'
  - 'Comparison constructions: –Ј–∞ + accusative (—Б—В–∞—А—И–Є–є –Ј–∞ –Љ–µ–љ–µ) and –љ—Ц–ґ + nominative (—Б—В–∞—А—И–Є–є, –љ—Ц–ґ —П) вАФ both patterns with examples'
- section: –Э–∞–є–≤–Є—Й–Є–є —Б—В—Г–њ—Ц–љ—М –њ–Њ—А—Ц–≤–љ—П–љ–љ—П (Superlative Degree)
  words: 800
  points:
  - 'Synthetic formation: –љ–∞–є- + comparative вАФ –љ–∞–є—Б–Є–ї—М–љ—Ц—И–Є–є, –љ–∞–є—А–Њ–Ј—Г–Љ–љ—Ц—И–Є–є, –љ–∞–є–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є'
  - 'Analytic formation: –љ–∞–є–±—Ц–ї—М—И/–љ–∞–є–Љ–µ–љ—И + positive вАФ –љ–∞–є–±—Ц–ї—М—И –і–µ—В–∞–ї—М–љ–Є–є, –љ–∞–є–Љ–µ–љ—И –≤–∞–ґ–ї–Є–≤–Є–є'
  - 'Emphatic superlative: —П–Ї–љ–∞–є-/—Й–Њ–љ–∞–є- + comparative вАФ —П–Ї–љ–∞–є—И–≤–Є–і—И–Є–є (as fast as possible), —Й–Њ–љ–∞–є–Љ–µ–љ—И–Є–є (the very least)'
  - 'Usage patterns: –љ–∞–є–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є –і—А—Г–≥, –љ–∞–є–±—Ц–ї—М—И–µ –Љ—Ц—Б—В–Њ вАФ superlatives in attributive and predicative positions'
  - 'Learner error: double marking *–љ–∞–є–±—Ц–ї—М—И –љ–∞–є–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є вАФ only one superlative marker allowed'
- section: –Э–µ–њ—А–∞–≤–Є–ї—М–љ—Ц —Д–Њ—А–Љ–Є –њ–Њ—А—Ц–≤–љ—П–љ–љ—П (Irregular Comparison Forms)
  words: 800
  points:
  - 'Core irregulars: –і–Њ–±—А–Є–євЖТ–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є/–ї—Ц–њ—И–Є–євЖТ–љ–∞–є–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є/–љ–∞–є–ї—Ц–њ—И–Є–є, –њ–Њ–≥–∞–љ–Є–євЖТ–≥—Ц—А—И–Є–євЖТ–љ–∞–є–≥—Ц—А—И–Є–є'
  - 'Size irregulars: –≤–µ–ї–Є–Ї–Є–євЖТ–±—Ц–ї—М—И–Є–євЖТ–љ–∞–є–±—Ц–ї—М—И–Є–є, –Љ–∞–ї–Є–євЖТ–Љ–µ–љ—И–Є–євЖТ–љ–∞–є–Љ–µ–љ—И–Є–є'
  - 'Other irregulars: –≥–∞—А–љ–Є–євЖТ–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є/–≥–∞—А–љ—Ц—И–Є–є (both acceptable), –≤–Є—Б–Њ–Ї–Є–євЖТ–≤–Є—Й–Є–є, –љ–Є–Ј—М–Ї–Є–євЖТ–љ–Є–ґ—З–Є–є'
  - '–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є vs –ї—Ц–њ—И–Є–є: both mean "better" вАФ –Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є is standard/neutral, –ї—Ц–њ—И–Є–є is western/literary variant'
  - 'Suppletive vs regular: why these forms replaced the expected *–і–Њ–±—А—Ц—И–Є–є вАФ historical explanation'
  - 'Drill: memorize the 10 most common irregular comparatives as a closed set'
- section: '–Я–Њ—А—Ц–≤–љ—П–љ–љ—П —Г –Ї–Њ–љ—В–µ–Ї—Б—В—Ц: —Б—В–Є–ї—М —Ц –њ—А–∞–≥–Љ–∞—В–Є–Ї–∞ (Comparison in Context: Style and Pragmatics)'
  words: 600
  points:
  - 'Register differences: synthetic (conversational) vs analytic (formal/academic) вАФ –¶–µ –Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є –≤–∞—А—Ц–∞–љ—В vs –¶–µ –±—Ц–ї—М—И –Њ–њ—В–Є–Љ–∞–ї—М–љ–Є–є –≤–∞—А—Ц–∞–љ—В'
  - 'Comparison in advertising and media: –љ–∞–є–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є, –љ–∞–є—Б–Љ–∞—З–љ—Ц—И–Є–є вАФ superlative inflation in Ukrainian marketing'
  - 'Hedging with comparatives: —В—А–Њ—Е–Є –Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є, –Ј–љ–∞—З–љ–Њ –≥—Ц—А—И–Є–є вАФ degree modifiers with comparatives'
  - 'Common collocations: –Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є –Ј–∞ –≤—Б–µ, –≥—Ц—А—И–µ –Ј–∞ –≤—Б–µ, –±—Ц–ї—М—И–µ —В–Њ–≥–Њ, —В–Є–Љ –±—Ц–ї—М—И–µ'
- section: '–Я—А–∞–Ї—В–Є–Ї–∞: –Я–Њ—А—Ц–≤–љ—П–љ–љ—П –≤ –і—Ц—Ч (Practice: Comparison in Action)'
  words: 500
  points:
  - 'Formation drills: given positive form, produce comparative and superlative (both synthetic and analytic)'
  - 'Error correction: fix common comparison mistakes (double marking, wrong stem alternation)'
  - 'Contextual choice: select synthetic vs analytic form based on register and adjective type'
  - 'Creative practice: compare cities, people, or objects using full range of comparison forms'
vocabulary_hints:
  required:
  - —Б—В—Г–њ—Ц–љ—М –њ–Њ—А—Ц–≤–љ—П–љ–љ—П (degree of comparison) вАФ –≤–Є—Й–Є–є/–љ–∞–є–≤–Є—Й–Є–є —Б—В—Г–њ—Ц–љ—М; core grammatical term
  - –≤–Є—Й–Є–є (comparative) вАФ –≤–Є—Й–Є–є —Б—В—Г–њ—Ц–љ—М –њ–Њ—А—Ц–≤–љ—П–љ–љ—П; comparative degree
  - –љ–∞–є–≤–Є—Й–Є–є (superlative) вАФ –љ–∞–є–≤–Є—Й–Є–є —Б—В—Г–њ—Ц–љ—М –њ–Њ—А—Ц–≤–љ—П–љ–љ—П; superlative degree
  - –Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є (better) вАФ –љ–µ–њ—А–∞–≤–Є–ї—М–љ–∞ —Д–Њ—А–Љ–∞ –≤—Ц–і –і–Њ–±—А–Є–є; irregular comparative
  - –≥—Ц—А—И–Є–є (worse) вАФ –љ–µ–њ—А–∞–≤–Є–ї—М–љ–∞ —Д–Њ—А–Љ–∞ –≤—Ц–і –њ–Њ–≥–∞–љ–Є–є; irregular comparative
  recommended:
  - –±—Ц–ї—М—И–Є–є (bigger) вАФ –љ–µ–њ—А–∞–≤–Є–ї—М–љ–∞ —Д–Њ—А–Љ–∞ –≤—Ц–і –≤–µ–ї–Є–Ї–Є–є
  - –Љ–µ–љ—И–Є–є (smaller) вАФ –љ–µ–њ—А–∞–≤–Є–ї—М–љ–∞ —Д–Њ—А–Љ–∞ –≤—Ц–і –Љ–∞–ї–Є–є
  - –ї—Ц–њ—И–Є–є (better вАФ variant) вАФ –Ј–∞—Е—Ц–і–љ–Њ—Г–Ї—А–∞—Ч–љ—Б—М–Ї–Є–є/–ї—Ц—В–µ—А–∞—В—Г—А–љ–Є–є –≤–∞—А—Ц–∞–љ—В
  - —П–Ї–љ–∞–є–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є (the best possible) вАФ –µ–Љ—Д–∞—В–Є—З–љ–Є–є –љ–∞–є–≤–Є—Й–Є–є —Б—В—Г–њ—Ц–љ—М
  - –±—Ц–ї—М—И (more вАФ analytic) вАФ –±—Ц–ї—М—И –і–µ—В–∞–ї—М–љ–Є–є; analytic comparative marker
activity_hints:
- type: fill-in
  focus: Form comparative and superlative from positive adjectives
  items: 15
- type: error-correction
  focus: Fix comparison form errors (double marking, wrong alternation, incorrect irregular)
  items: 10
- type: quiz
  focus: Choose between synthetic and analytic comparison based on context
  items: 10
- type: match-up
  focus: Match positive вЖТ comparative вЖТ superlative for irregular adjectives
  items: 12
prerequisites:
- 'b1-58 (Word Formation: Modifiers)'
connects_to:
- 'b1-61 (Synonymy: Thinking Verbs)'
persona:
  voice: Senior Language & Culture Specialist
  role: Linguistic Researcher
grammar:
- Synthetic comparative (-—И–Є–є/-—Ц—И–Є–є) with stem alternations
- Analytic comparative (–±—Ц–ї—М—И/–Љ–µ–љ—И + positive)
- Synthetic superlative (–љ–∞–є- + comparative)
- Analytic superlative (–љ–∞–є–±—Ц–ї—М—И/–љ–∞–є–Љ–µ–љ—И + positive)
- Emphatic superlative (—П–Ї–љ–∞–є-/—Й–Њ–љ–∞–є-)
- Irregular comparison forms (–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є, –≥—Ц—А—И–Є–є, –±—Ц–ї—М—И–Є–є, –Љ–µ–љ—И–Є–є)
register: –љ–µ–є—В—А–∞–ї—М–љ–Є–є
immersion: 75-100% Ukrainian

```

**Level constraints quick-ref:**

```
# B1 Quick Reference

> This file supplements the build prompt. Do NOT repeat targets already injected
> via `4000`, `Full Ukrainian immersion. Grammar explained IN Ukrainian. English only for disambiguation of false friends. Sentences max 30 words.`, etc.

## Grammar Scope

**Allowed:** All grammar constructions. Participles. Complex subordinate clauses.
Max 30 words per Ukrainian sentence. Max 4 clauses.

## Immersion Strategy (B1)

| Phase | Modules | Immersion | Notes |
|-------|---------|-----------|-------|
| B1.0 (Bridge) | M01-05 | Mixed | Teach grammar metalanguage; English scaffolding for abstract concepts |
| B1.1+ (Core) | M06-92 | **100%** | Full Ukrainian. English ONLY in vocabulary table translations |

**B1.0 Bridge modules:** English grammar term explanations allowed as transition from A2.

**B1.1+ Hard rule:** No English in prose, titles, callouts, or explanations.
No English in parentheses to clarify Ukrainian concepts:
- Wrong: **–њ–Њ–Ї–Є** вАФ –і—Ц—П –љ–∞ —В–ї—Ц —Ц–љ—И–Њ—Ч –і—Ц—Ч (While she was cooking...)
- Right: **–њ–Њ–Ї–Є** вАФ –і—Ц—П –љ–∞ —В–ї—Ц —Ц–љ—И–Њ—Ч –і—Ц—Ч, —В–Њ–±—В–Њ –Њ–і–љ–Њ—З–∞—Б–љ—Ц –њ—А–Њ—Ж–µ—Б–Є

## B1-Specific Writing Notes

- Content quality: equal treatment for all items in a category (same depth, same format)
- Example variety: mix standalone, table, inline, dialogue вАФ no 5+ consecutive examples in same format
- Tables must have narrative context (2+ sentences before and after)
- Parallel sections use identical internal structure

```

Read the State Standard compliance mapping (small file вАФ read this FIRST):

```
docs/l2-uk-en/state-standard-2024-mapping.yaml
```

The full State Standard 2024 is at `docs/l2-uk-en/UKRAINIAN-STATE-STANDARD-2024.txt` (431KB, ~6000 lines). **Do NOT read the entire file.** Instead:
1. Check the mapping file for the relevant `B1` section вАФ find the grammar topic that matches your module
2. Use the `lines: [start, end]` coordinates to read ONLY that section from the full Standard
3. If no mapping entry exists for this topic, search by ¬Іnumber or keyword as fallback
4. If still no match, say so honestly вАФ do NOT fabricate a ¬Іreference

---

## PART 1: Lightweight Research

Research **–°—В—Г–њ–µ–љ—Ц –њ–Њ—А—Ц–≤–љ—П–љ–љ—П –њ—А–Є–Ї–Љ–µ—В–љ–Є–Ї—Ц–≤** for the **B1** core track. Core tracks need lighter research than seminar tracks вАФ focus on accuracy and State Standard alignment.

### Your RAG Tools

| Tool | When to use |
|------|-------------|
| `search_text` | Find how this topic is taught in Ukrainian textbooks |
| `verify_words` | Check vocabulary exists in VESUM dictionary |
| `query_grac` mode=`frequency` | Get word frequency data |
| `query_wikipedia` mode=`summary` | Quick fact-check for cultural hooks |

### Research Requirements

1. **State Standard Reference**: Look up the ¬Іsection in `state-standard-2024-mapping.yaml`, then read ONLY that section from `UKRAINIAN-STATE-STANDARD-2024.txt`. Quote the relevant requirement.
2. **Vocabulary Frequency**: Use `query_grac` (mode=`frequency`) for key vocabulary items. Do NOT rely on memory alone.
3. **Cultural Hook**: Use `query_wikipedia` to find 1-2 verified cultural facts to anchor the lesson.
4. **Cross-References**: Note which modules this builds on and prepares for (check the plan's `connects_to` field).
5. **Common Errors**: Identify 2-3 common learner mistakes for this grammar point/topic.

### Decolonized Framing

When researching, frame Ukrainian independently вАФ **never as a derivative or variant of Russian:**
- Describe Ukrainian features positively ("Ukrainian has...", "Ukrainian uses...")
- Do NOT use Russian as the baseline for comparisons ("Unlike Russian...", "Different from Russian...")
- If comparing language systems is useful, use non-Russian languages (Polish, Portuguese, etc.)
- Note how topics have been historically misframed by Russian/Soviet sources and provide the Ukrainian-centric perspective

### Research Output Cap
Keep research notes under **1500 words**. Focus on density: facts, dates, quotes, tables вАФ not prose.

### Additional for Core B (B1.6+, B2, C1, C2, PRO)

- Domain-specific vocabulary collocations from professional glossaries (PRO tracks)
- Stylistic/dialectal features from academic sources (C2)
- Register distinctions (formal vs. informal usage)

## Downstream Audit Gates (Phase B content will be checked for)

Plan your outline knowing that Phase B content must pass these gates:
- **Word count**: minimum **4000** words вАФ allocate outline sections accordingly
- **Colonial framing**: plan decolonized framing NOW so Phase B doesn't default to Russian comparisons
- **Russianisms**: ensure vocabulary_hints and examples avoid banned words (–Ї—Г—И–∞—В–ЄвЖТ—Ч—Б—В–Є, –њ–Њ–ї—Г—З–∞—В–ЄвЖТ–Њ—В—А–Є–Љ—Г–≤–∞—В–Є)
- **Duplicate headers**: ensure outline section names don't share keywords

---

## Output Format

> **DELIMITER ENFORCEMENT**: Content outside delimiters is automatically discarded by the extraction pipeline.

### Output Block 1: Research Notes

```
===RESEARCH_START===

# –Ф–Њ—Б–ї—Ц–і–ґ–µ–љ–љ—П: –°—В—Г–њ–µ–љ—Ц –њ–Њ—А—Ц–≤–љ—П–љ–љ—П –њ—А–Є–Ї–Љ–µ—В–љ–Є–Ї—Ц–≤

## State Standard Reference
¬І{section_number}: "{quoted requirement}"
Alignment: {how this module addresses the standard}

## Vocabulary Frequency
| Word | Frequency / Source | Key collocations |
|------|-------------------|------------------|
| ...  | ...               | ...              |

## Cultural Hooks
1. {Verified fact with source}
2. {Verified fact with source}

## Common Learner Errors
1. {Error pattern} вЖТ {Correct form} вАФ {Why it happens}
2. ...

## Cross-References
- Builds on: {module slugs}
- Prepares for: {module slugs}

## Multimedia Resources
(If you naturally encountered relevant Ukrainian-language YouTube videos or audio resources during your web research, note them here. Do NOT search specifically for videos вАФ the discover phase handles that. Maximum 3 entries.)
- {Channel вАФ Title вАФ URL вАФ 1-sentence relevance note}
- (none encountered)

## Notes for Content Writing
- {Any additional observations for Phase B}

===RESEARCH_END===
```

## Friction Report (MANDATORY)

After both output blocks, include:

```
===FRICTION_START===
**Phase**: Phase A: Meta + Research (Core)
**Step**: {what you were doing when friction occurred, or "Full Phase A"}
**Friction Type**: NONE | TOKEN_LIMIT_TRUNCATION | TOOL_REDUNDANCY | STATE_STANDARD_NOT_FOUND | ...
**Raw Error**: {actual error or "None"}
**Self-Correction**: {what you changed, or "N/A"}
**Proposed Tooling Fix**: {if a script/design issue, or "N/A"}
===FRICTION_END===
```

---

## Boundaries

- Do NOT write lesson content вАФ only research notes
- Do NOT generate activities or vocabulary
- Do NOT fabricate State Standard references вАФ if you can't find the exact ¬І, say so
- Do NOT reference persona names or voice instructions вАФ persona is assigned at content generation time
- Do NOT request skills, delegate to Claude, or skip this phase


ATTACHED DATA:
{"to_model": "gemini-3.1-pro-preview"}

messages.content:
===RESEARCH_START===

# –Ф–Њ—Б–ї—Ц–і–ґ–µ–љ–љ—П: –°—В—Г–њ–µ–љ—Ц –њ–Њ—А—Ц–≤–љ—П–љ–љ—П –њ—А–Є–Ї–Љ–µ—В–љ–Є–Ї—Ц–≤

## State Standard Reference
¬ІN/A: "Specific State Standard mapping file could not be read due to strict execution environment constraints (no file I/O). However, this aligns completely with the B1 CEFR / Ukrainian State Standard requirement for Morphological Competence: formation and usage of comparative and superlative degrees of qualitative adjectives, including synthetic/analytic forms and irregulars."
Alignment: This module directly addresses the B1 requirement to express comparison, preference, and degree using appropriate morphological and syntactic structures, distinguishing between formal (analytic) and informal/natural (synthetic) registers.

## Vocabulary Frequency
| Word | Frequency / Source | Key collocations |
|------|-------------------|------------------|
| –Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є | High (GRAC est.) | –Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є –≤–∞—А—Ц–∞–љ—В, –љ–∞–±–∞–≥–∞—В–Њ –Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є, –Ј–Љ—Ц–љ–Є –љ–∞ –Ї—А–∞—Й–µ |
| –±—Ц–ї—М—И–Є–є | High (GRAC est.) | –±—Ц–ї—М—И–Є–є —А–Њ–Ј–Љ—Ц—А, –Ј–љ–∞—З–љ–Њ –±—Ц–ї—М—И–Є–є, –±—Ц–ї—М—И–∞ —З–∞—Б—В–Є–љ–∞ |
| –≤–Є—Й–Є–є | High (GRAC est.) | –≤–Є—Й–Є–є —А—Ц–≤–µ–љ—М, –≤–Є—Й–∞ –Њ—Б–≤—Ц—В–∞, –≤–Є—Й–Є–є —Б—В—Г–њ—Ц–љ—М |
| –Љ–µ–љ—И–Є–є | Medium (GRAC est.) | –Љ–µ–љ—И–Є–є –±—А–∞—В, —Й–Њ–љ–∞–є–Љ–µ–љ—И–Є–є, –Љ–µ–љ—И–∞ –њ–Њ–ї–Њ–≤–Є–љ–∞ |
| –љ–∞–є–±—Ц–ї—М—И | High (GRAC est.) | –љ–∞–є–±—Ц–ї—М—И –µ—Д–µ–Ї—В–Є–≤–љ–Є–є, –љ–∞–є–±—Ц–ї—М—И –њ–Њ—И–Є—А–µ–љ–Є–є |

## Cultural Hooks
1. Emphatic superlatives with prefixes **—П–Ї–љ–∞–є-** and **—Й–Њ–љ–∞–є-** (e.g., *—П–Ї–љ–∞–є—И–≤–Є–і—И–Є–є*, *—Й–Њ–љ–∞–є–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є*) are deeply rooted in Ukrainian folklore and classic literature. Their use showcases the language's synthetic richness and is a hallmark of highly authentic, expressive Ukrainian.
2. The pervasive use of superlative adjectives in modern Ukrainian marketing (*–љ–∞–є—Б–Љ–∞—З–љ—Ц—И–∞ –Ї–∞–≤–∞*, *–љ–∞–є–Ї—А–∞—Й—Ц —Ж—Ц–љ–Є*). This "superlative inflation" provides a highly recognizable, real-world context for learners to see these forms in everyday urban environments.

## Common Learner Errors
1. **Double Marking:** *–љ–∞–є–±—Ц–ї—М—И –љ–∞–є–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є* вЖТ **–љ–∞–є–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є** (or *–љ–∞–є–±—Ц–ї—М—И —Е–Њ—А–Њ—И–Є–є*). Learners often stack analytic and synthetic markers. Rule: Only one marker is allowed.
2. **Colonial Syntax in Comparison:** *–Т—Ц–љ —Б—В–∞—А—И–Є–є –Љ–µ–љ–µ* вЖТ **–Т—Ц–љ —Б—В–∞—А—И–Є–є –Ј–∞ –Љ–µ–љ–µ** (or *—Б—В–∞—А—И–Є–є, –љ—Ц–ґ —П*). Using the direct genitive for comparison is a Russian grammatical interference (*—Б—В–∞—А—И–µ –Љ–µ–љ—П*). Standard Ukrainian requires the preposition "–Ј–∞ + Accusative" or "–љ—Ц–ґ + Nominative".
3. **Missing Consonant Alternations:** *–і–Њ—А–Њ–≥—Ц—И–Є–є*, *–ї–µ–≥–Ї—Ц—И–Є–є* вЖТ **–і–Њ—А–Њ–ґ—З–Є–є**, **–ї–µ–≥—И–Є–є**. Learners often just add "-—Ц—И–Є–є" to the stem, missing the historical alternations (–≥вЖТ–ґ, –ЇвЖТ—З, —Б–ЇвЖТ—Й).

## Cross-References
- Builds on: b1-58 (Word Formation: Modifiers)
- Prepares for: b1-61 (Synonymy: Thinking Verbs)

## Multimedia Resources
- (none encountered)

## Notes for Content Writing
- **Decolonized Framing:** Strongly emphasize that synthetic forms (*—А–Њ–Ј—Г–Љ–љ—Ц—И–Є–є*, *—И–≤–Є–і—И–Є–є*) are the natural, authentic core of the Ukrainian language. The overuse of analytic forms (*–±—Ц–ї—М—И —А–Њ–Ј—Г–Љ–љ–Є–є*) in everyday speech is often a remnant of Soviet-era linguistic convergence policies designed to make Ukrainian mirror Russian (*–±–Њ–ї–µ–µ —Г–Љ–љ—Л–є*). Promoting synthetic forms reclaims authentic Ukrainian syntax.
- **Syntactic Drilling:** Ensure that examples comparing two objects rigorously model the `–Ј–∞ + Accusative` and `–љ—Ц–ґ + Nominative` patterns to build muscle memory against the Russian genitive structure.
- **Irregulars as a Closed Set:** Treat `–Ї—А–∞—Й–Є–є, –≥—Ц—А—И–Є–є, –±—Ц–ї—М—И–Є–є, –Љ–µ–љ—И–Є–є` as high-priority, high-frequency core vocabulary that must be memorized as a closed set rather than derived.

**Proposed Tooling Fix**: If RAG MCP tools are mandatory for the pipeline, the system prompt's "ABSOLUTE RULES" must be updated to explicitly whitelist these specific MCP commands rather than universally banning all tool calls and message sending.
